metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add a repo-checkable proving step for AC5 (e.g. a `discovered_from` reference in a plan metadata file the CI can assert on), or explicitly reframe AC5 as a process checklist item outside the code ACs so it is not mistaken for a testable criterion.
findings.finding: AC5 ('The split-out landmine detector is captured as `discovered_from` follow-up `acoustic-coveting-turaco`') has no in-session proving command — verifying a story-link exists in an external tracker is not achievable via repo artifacts or CI, making this criterion not evaluable in-session.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — AC5: 'The split-out landmine detector is captured as `discovered_from` follow-up `acoustic-coveting-turaco` (no detector code ships in this story).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit guard in the demotion rule (and its unit test) specifying that a finding with an empty `criteria` list is NOT demoted (i.e. the all-criteria predicate requires `len(criteria) > 0`).
findings.finding: The all-criteria demotion rule has an unspecified vacuous-truth edge: Python's `all([])` is `True`, so a finding with `criteria=[]` (no criteria at all) trivially satisfies 'ALL criteria are nit-suppressed' and would be silently demoted. The plan and its ACs never address this case.
findings.suggested_fix: In workflow_ops.py's demotion loop: `crits = f.get('criteria') or []; if crits and all(c in nit_suppressed for c in crits): ...`. Add an AC-level test: 'an advisory finding with criteria=[] still surfaces'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default for the empty-criteria edge case in the 'What' section (e.g. 'a finding with no criteria is treated as non-suppressed and surfaces normally').
findings.finding: The all-criteria rule for nit-suppression demotion has an unspecified vacuous-truth edge case: a finding with an empty criteria set trivially satisfies 'ALL criteria are nit-suppressed', so it would be silently demoted. The plan states no default for this case, leaving the implementer to decide.
findings.location: ## What — 'AFTER `pass3_over_findings` returns the decided findings, the partition loop re-buckets any finding whose `decision == "advisory"` and whose criteria are ALL nit-suppressed into `dropped`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the What section: 'A finding whose criteria list is empty (or absent) is treated as non-suppressed and always surfaces.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether nit-demoted findings have their `decision` field overwritten to `'dropped'` (in addition to `reason`) before being appended to the `dropped` bucket, so `decided` stays self-consistent with the partition.
findings.finding: The plan's nit-suppression demotion overwrites a finding's `reason` to `'nit-suppressed'` in the `dropped` bucket, but does NOT specify whether the same finding's `decision` field (set by the kernel to `'advisory'`) is also overwritten when the finding is moved from `surfaced` to `dropped`. The `decided` list (which is returned alongside the buckets) would then contain a finding with `decision='advisory'` that is NOT in `surfaced` — an inconsistency between the `decided` flat list and the partitioned buckets.
findings.location: What section — 'the partition loop re-buckets … overwriting its `reason` field'; `workflow_ops.py` lines 306-322

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify and specify what value `decision` carries in the `decided` list for nit-suppressed findings: either (a) also rewrite `decision` to `'dropped'` in `decided` so the top-level output is internally consistent, or (b) explicitly document that `decided` always reflects the raw kernel verdict and consumers must use `surfaced`/`dropped` for the final gate-side disposition.
findings.finding: The `decided` top-level output will contain an inconsistent state for nit-suppressed findings: they will carry `decision='advisory'` (the kernel's unmodified verdict) but `reason='nit-suppressed'` (mutated by the partition loop), while being absent from `surfaced` and present in `dropped`. A consumer reading `decided` directly — rather than the `surfaced`/`dropped` partition outputs — will see a finding labelled advisory that is actually suppressed, with no `decision` field update to confirm the demotion. The plan is silent on whether `decision` should also be rewritten to `'dropped'` in the `decided` list for nit-suppressed findings.
findings.location: What section — 'the partition loop re-buckets any finding whose decision == "advisory" and whose criteria are ALL nit-suppressed into dropped INSTEAD of surfaced, overwriting its reason field'
findings.suggested_fix: Preferred: when re-bucketing a nit-suppressed finding to `dropped`, also update `f['decision'] = 'dropped'` in the same mutation step (or shallow-copy the finding before mutating to avoid aliasing surprises). This makes `decided` self-consistent: every entry's `decision` field matches which bucket it lands in.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify (in the What section or the `code_review_decide` step description) whether the `decided` list is mutated in-place to reflect the nit-suppressed demotion, or whether `decided` is redefined as the post-demotion union of all four buckets, and add an AC assertion covering `decided` list consistency.
findings.finding: The plan says the demotion loop 'overwriting its `reason` field' and re-buckets the finding from `surfaced` to `dropped`, but does not specify whether the mutation is also reflected in the `decided` list. The current `code_review_decide` returns BOTH `decided` (the full flat list from `pass3_over_findings`) AND the partition buckets. If `decided` is not updated, a downstream consumer reading `decided` will see `decision='advisory'` for a finding that appears only in `dropped`, creating a structural inconsistency.
findings.location: What section (demotion rule) / Scope: workflow_ops.py `code_review_decide` / code_review_decide_output schema

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `code_review_decide` should pass `ctx.repo_root` to `nit_suppressed_criteria` (making the overlay-aware contract real at the call-site), or narrow the spec to packaged-index-only and drop the 'overlay-aware' claim from the helper description.
findings.finding: The plan names `nit_suppressed_criteria(routing_map=None)` as 'overlay-aware, mirroring `threshold_for`/`effective_routing`', but `code_review_decide` — the only named call-site — currently threads NO `repo_root` into its routing calls (it uses the packaged index via `registry.threshold_for` directly). The plan does not specify how the overlay-aware version of `nit_suppressed_criteria` gets a `repo_root` at the call-site in `code_review_decide`, creating a gap between the stated overlay-aware contract and the reachable call-site.
findings.suggested_fix: In `code_review_decide`, call `registry.nit_suppressed_criteria(routing_map=registry.effective_routing(ctx.repo_root))` so project overlays are honoured — consistent with how `threshold_for` is designed to accept an optional routing_map.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `code_review_decide` passes `effective_routing(ctx.repo_root)` (not the packaged default) to `nit_suppressed_criteria`, consistent with how other overlay-aware helpers are consumed in the codebase.
findings.finding: The plan specifies `nit_suppressed_criteria` should be overlay-aware (mirroring `threshold_for` / `effective_routing`), but does not specify whether `code_review_decide`'s nit-suppression check uses the packaged routing index or `effective_routing(ctx.repo_root)`. The existing `threshold_for` call in `code_review_decide` uses the packaged index by default; if `nit_suppressed_criteria` is called without the repo-aware routing map, a project overlay that adds `nit_suppressed: false` to `docs` or `llm-prompts` as a re-tune would be silently ignored — the suppression would fire even when the project overrides it.
findings.location: Scope / files — `registry.py` — 'a `nit_suppressed_criteria(routing_map=None)` helper returning the set of criteria flagged `nit_suppressed` (overlay-aware, mirroring `threshold_for` / `effective_routing`)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the `code_review_decide` implementation spec, add: call `registry.nit_suppressed_criteria(routing_map=registry.effective_routing(ctx.repo_root))` to honour project overlays — parallel to how `threshold_for` accepts a `routing_map` argument for repo-aware callers.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback note to the plan (e.g., 'to revert, set `nit_suppressed: false` for the affected entries in `criteria_routing.json` and redeploy') so the reversibility mechanism is stated, not assumed.
findings.finding: The plan changes existing advisory-surfacing behavior (docs/llm-prompts findings previously surfaced will now be dropped) but does not state an explicit rollback or back-out mechanism. T4 requires a reversibility mechanism, not just acknowledgement of the change.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-sentence rollback note under the Scope or What section: 'Rollback: set `"nit_suppressed": false` on the affected routing entries and redeploy — no data migration required.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a doc update (CLAUDE.md entry, an ADR, or an inline schema comment in `criteria_routing.json` + a comment in `code_review_decide`) capturing: (a) the `nit_suppressed` flag semantics and all-criteria rule, (b) the `"nit-suppressed"` reason string as a new member of the decide-reason vocabulary, and (c) how future criteria can opt in.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new behavioral contract — the `nit_suppressed` config flag, the all-criteria suppression rule, and the canonical `"nit-suppressed"` reason string — with no planned update to any ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design doc. This is an extension point (future criteria could be flagged) and a new entry in the `code_review_decide` reason vocabulary, both of which future maintainers need to discover.
findings.location: Scope/files section; AC line 1 (`nit_suppressed: true`); What section (`"nit-suppressed"` reason vocabulary); no doc-update file listed anywhere in Scope/files
findings.suggested_fix: At minimum, add a `// nit_suppressed: true — advisory findings whose criteria are ALL nit-suppressed are demoted to dropped (all-criteria rule; see code_review_decide)` comment block in `criteria_routing.json` alongside the flag, and add a one-paragraph note to CLAUDE.md (or the existing design doc for `code_review_decide`) documenting the reason-vocabulary extension. An ADR is warranted if `criteria_routing.json` is already treated as a documented extension point.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate (or confirm the absence of) downstream consumers of the `dropped` partition's `reason` field and assert they either do not switch on reason strings or explicitly handle the new `"nit-suppressed"` value.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new `reason` value (`"nit-suppressed"`) into the `dropped` partition of `code_review_decide` but does not enumerate or audit downstream consumers of the `dropped` list to confirm they tolerate an unknown reason string. If any reporting, analytics, audit, or display layer pattern-matches on the reason vocabulary of dropped findings, the new value could cause silent misclassification or an unhandled branch.
findings.location: ## What — 'overwriting its `reason` field with the canonical string `"nit-suppressed"`' and AC item 2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short 'consumers of `dropped`' note to the ## What section: either (a) state that no downstream consumer reads `dropped[*].reason` and therefore no change is needed, or (b) list the consumers and confirm they treat unknown reason values as pass-through/ignored.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `nit_suppressed_criteria` needs to be a named, exported registry helper with an overlay-aware `routing_map=None` signature, or whether the suppression lookup can be inlined into `code_review_decide` (a one-liner set comprehension over `routing_index().items()`) since this story names only one production call-site.
findings.finding: The proposed `nit_suppressed_criteria(routing_map=None)` registry helper has only one named production call-site in this story's done-definition (`code_review_decide` in `workflow_ops.py`), which does not meet the Rule-of-Three bar for a named, exported abstraction. The `routing_map=None` optional parameter signals anticipation of future overlay-aware callers (mirroring `threshold_for`), but no such caller exists in scope — making the parameter itself speculative. A one-liner set comprehension inlined into `code_review_decide` (e.g. `{cid for cid, e in routing_index().items() if (e or {}).get('nit_suppressed')}`) would satisfy the AC without premature abstraction.
findings.suggested_fix: Inline the lookup directly in `code_review_decide`: `nit_set = {cid for cid, e in (routing_map or registry.routing_index()).items() if (e or {}).get('nit_suppressed')}`. If a second call-site emerges in a follow-up story, extract the helper then (Rule-of-Three deferred).

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
